endpoints.name: Get a list of personal access tokens
endpoints.description: Returns a paginated list of personal access tokens.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Create a personal access token
endpoints.description: Creates and returns a personal access token.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Get a personal access token
endpoints.description: Returns a personal access token by UUID.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{UUID}} with a real uuid.

endpoints.name: Delete a personal access token
endpoints.description: Deletes a personal access token permanently. This cannot be undone.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{UUID}} with a real uuid.

endpoints.name: Update a personal access token
endpoints.description:
Updates a personal access token partially. You can either update the
token's label or enable/disable it.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{UUID}} with a real uuid.

endpoints.name: Returns list of audit log  events.
endpoints.description: Get audit log events for a given namespace.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ACCOUNT}} with a real account.

endpoints.name: Returns list of audit log actions.
endpoints.description: Get audit log actions for a namespace to be used as a filter for querying audit events.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ACCOUNT}} with a real account.

endpoints.name: Delete images
endpoints.description:
Deletes one or more images within a namespace. This is currently limited to a single 
repository.

If you attempt to delete images that are marked as active or are currently tagged, the deletion does not happen and it displays the warnings.
To continue with the deletion, you must ignore these warnings by putting them in the `ignore_warnings` property.

Deleting a currently tagged image deletes the tag from the repository.

You cannot ignore errors. It is not possible to directly delete children of multi-arch images.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{NAMESPACE}} with a real namespace.

endpoints.name: Get details of repository's images
endpoints.description: Gets details on the images in a repository.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{NAMESPACE}} with a real namespace. Replace {{REPOSITORY}} with a real repository.

endpoints.name: Get summary of repository's images
endpoints.description:
Gets the number of images in a repository and the number of images
counted as active and inactive.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{NAMESPACE}} with a real namespace. Replace {{REPOSITORY}} with a real repository.

endpoints.name: Get image's tags
endpoints.description: Gets current and historical tags for an image.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{DIGEST}} with a real digest. Replace {{NAMESPACE}} with a real namespace. Replace {{REPOSITORY}} with a real repository.

endpoints.name: List repository tags
endpoints.description: List repository tags
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{NAMESPACE}} with a real namespace. Replace {{REPOSITORY}} with a real repository.

endpoints.name: Check repository tags
endpoints.description: Check repository tags
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{NAMESPACE}} with a real namespace. Replace {{REPOSITORY}} with a real repository.

endpoints.name: Read repository tag
endpoints.description: Read repository tag
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{NAMESPACE}} with a real namespace. Replace {{TAG}} with a real tag. Replace {{REPOSITORY}} with a real repository.

endpoints.name: Check repository tag
endpoints.description: Check repository tag
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{NAMESPACE}} with a real namespace. Replace {{TAG}} with a real tag. Replace {{REPOSITORY}} with a real repository.

endpoints.name: Get organization settings
endpoints.description: Returns organization settings by name.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: Update organization settings
endpoints.description:
Updates an organization's settings. Some settings are only used when the
organization is on a business plan.

***Only users in the "owners" group of the organization can use this endpoint.***

The following settings are only used on a business plan:
- `restricted_images`
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: List resource types
endpoints.description: Returns all resource types supported for the SCIM configuration.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Get a resource type
endpoints.description: Returns a resource type by name.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{NAME}} with a real name.

endpoints.name: List schemas
endpoints.description: Returns all schemas supported for the SCIM configuration.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Get a schema
endpoints.description: Returns a schema by ID.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get service provider config
endpoints.description: Returns a service provider config for Docker's configuration.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: List users
endpoints.description:
List users, returns paginated users for an organization. Use `startIndex` and `count` query parameters to receive paginated results. **Sorting:**
Sorting lets you to specify the order of returned resources by specifying a combination of `sortBy` and `sortOrder` query parameters. The `sortBy` parameter specifies the attribute whose value will be used to order the returned responses. The `sortOrder` parameter defines the order in which the `sortBy` parameter is applied. Allowed values are "ascending" and "descending". **Filtering:**
You can request a subset of resources by specifying the `filter` query parameter containing a filter expression. Attribute names and attribute operators used in filters are case insensitive. The filter parameter must contain at least one valid expression. Each expression must contain an attribute name followed by an attribute operator and an optional value. Supported operators are listed below. - `eq` equal - `ne` not equal - `co` contains - `sw` starts with - `and` Logical "and" - `or` Logical "or" - `not` "Not" function - `()` Precedence grouping
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Create user
endpoints.description:
Creates a user. If the user already exists by email, they are assigned
to the organization on the "company" team.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.

endpoints.name: Get a user
endpoints.description: Returns a user by ID.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Update a user
endpoints.description:
Updates a user. Use this route to change the user's name, activate,
and deactivate the user.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Create an authentication token
endpoints.description:
Creates and returns a bearer token in JWT format that you can use to
authenticate with Docker Hub APIs.

The returned token is used in the HTTP Authorization header like `Authorization: Bearer {TOKEN}`.

Most Docker Hub APIs require this token either to consume or to get detailed information. For example, to list images in a private repository.
endpoints.notes: No authentication required.
